title: artemisia genipi subsp. eriantha
intro: Artemisia genipi subsp. eriantha, also known as sweet wormwood, is a subspecies of the wormwood plant, native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimalarial, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective against various infections and diseases. The health benefits of sweet wormwood include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of malaria, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Sweet wormwood can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments, and it is also sometimes used as an ingredient in traditional remedies and folk medicine.
